\rhead{\small RSCS Foundations}

\title{\vspace{-2.0em}\textbf{RSCS 1.0 Foundations:\\
A Typed Coordinate and Operator Framework\\
as a Conservative Extension of RGCS~v2}}
\author{Andrew Green}
\date{2026}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
The Resonant Spacetime Coordinate System (RSCS~1.0) is a typed
mathematical layer above the frozen RGCS~v2.0.0 crystal-application core:
\gvNumCoords{} coordinate types on declared manifolds and \gvNumOps{}
operators with units, classification, source provenance, and machine
tests. Its defining discipline is the \emph{Conservative Extension
Property}: for every operator that generalizes a frozen v2 result,
$O_{\mathrm{RSCS}}(\iotamap(x)) = \iotamap(O_{\mathrm{RGCS}}(x))$ within
declared tolerance on the v2 domain, so the extension can never silently
change v2 (battery status: \gvCepAllOk). Classification tags (\EST{}
established, \DER{} derived, \HYP{} hypothesis, \SRC{} source claim,
\ENG{} engineering) attach to every definition, and a machine-enforced
firewall forbids strong claims built from weak inputs. RSCS provides
models and falsification protocols; it asserts no unconfirmed physics.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
RGCS~v2 established a deterministic, tested crystal-resonance core with
strict claim classification. Version~3 asks a structural question: can
the same discipline support a broader family of coordinates (phase,
polarization, orientation, group delay, provenance) and operators
(coupling, propagation, preparation, observation) without weakening the
frozen baseline? RSCS answers with three rules:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Typed states.} Every state is an immutable coordinate
record on a declared manifold ($\mathbb{E}^3$, $S^1$, $SO(3)$,
$\mathbb{C}^n$, \dots) validated at construction; flat untyped vectors
are non-compliant (D3-009).
\item \textbf{Conservative extension.} New operators must reproduce the
frozen results they generalize (Section~\ref{sec:cep}).
\item \textbf{Provenance as data.} Classification and source identity
travel with the state; a machine firewall (D3-012) rejects any
\EST/\DER{} output computed from \HYP/\ENG/\SRC{} inputs without an
explicit downgrade.
\end{enumerate}
The framework \emph{earns its complexity} case by case: wherever a
simpler model predicts equally well, the registry says so and the simple
model wins (v2 rule, inherited).

  typed, immutable, unit-tagged};
\node[box, right=of coords] (ops) {Operators \eqid{RSCS-O.1--23}\\
  units + class + provenance + test};
\node[box, below=of coords] (reg) {Machine registry\\
  \texttt{rscs\_registry.yaml}};
\node[box, below=of ops] (fw) {Classification firewall\\
  no strong-from-weak (D3-012)};
\node[box, below right=7mm and -14mm of reg] (emb)
  {Embedding $\iotamap$ + CEP battery\\ reproduces frozen RGCS-M.$\ast$};
\node[box, below=of emb, fill=white] (v2)
  {\textbf{RGCS v2.0.0 (frozen)}\\ \texttt{rgcs\_core}, tag
  \texttt{v2.0.0}, archive};
\draw[arr] (coords) -- (ops);
\draw[arr] (coords) -- (reg);
\draw[arr] (ops) -- (fw);
\draw[arr] (reg) -- (emb);
\draw[arr] (fw) -- (emb);
\draw[arr] (emb) -- node[right,font=\scriptsize]
  {$O_{\mathrm{RSCS}}\circ\iotamap = \iotamap\circ O_{\mathrm{RGCS}}$}
  (v2);
\end{tikzpicture}
\caption{State/operator architecture. The frozen v2 core is never
modified; RSCS reaches it only through the embedding, and the CEP battery
pins the round trip.}
\label{fig:arch}
\end{figure}

\section{Coordinates}
\input{tables/coordinates}
Coordinates reject non-finite components at construction, wrap periodic
quantities onto $S^1$, and normalize where the manifold demands it (the
polarization state \eqid{RSCS-C.9} lives on the Poincar\'e sphere and
carries exact Jones$\leftrightarrow$Stokes conversions). The
memory-lattice coordinate \eqid{RSCS-C.14} is \HYP{} and quarantined: it
cannot be constructed without an explicit
\texttt{acknowledge\_hypothesis} flag (D3-013).

\section{Operators}
\input{tables/operators}

\subsection{The coupling keystone \texorpdfstring{\eqid{RSCS-O.4}}{RSCS-O.4}}
The time-domain coupling of a degenerate mode pair is anti-Hermitian,
\begin{equation}
K = i\,2\pi g, \qquad K^\dagger = -K, \qquad \eqid{RSCS-O.4}\ \EST
\end{equation}
inheriting the v2 QA correction QA-D-04 and frozen by D3-007. The
frequency-domain Hermitian matrix $H=\operatorname{diag}(f)+g$ yields
hybrid frequencies $f_0 \pm g$: for the golden case
($f_A=f_B=\SI{1000}{\hertz}$, $g=\SI{10}{\hertz}$) the operators return
\gvHybridLower{} and \gvHybridUpper{}~\si{\hertz}, a splitting of
\gvSplitting{}~\si{\hertz} ($=2g$), matching frozen RGCS-M.24 exactly.
The forbidden real-symmetric $K = \pi g$ grows without bound and is
pinned as a contrast regression test. The Autler--Townes dressed
response \eqid{RSCS-O.20} \EST{} maps onto the same algebra with
$G = 2\pi(2g)$~\citep{sohn2021phonon}; Figure~\ref{fig:ats} shows the
weak/strong regimes.

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.72\linewidth]{ats_lineshape}
\caption{Two-Lorentzian dressed lineshape \eqid{RSCS-O.20}: a single peak
for $G\ll\kappa$, a resolved doublet split by $G$ for $G\gg\kappa$
(threshold $G>\sqrt{\kappa_1\kappa_2}$). Lineshape algebra only; no
device physics is imported \SRC.}
\label{fig:ats}
\end{figure}

\subsection{Adapted mathematics and the firewall}
Operators O.6--O.9 and O.18--O.23 adapt published mathematics
(transfer-matrix cascades, phase matching, group-delay balancing,
state-dependent susceptibility, directional propagation)
\citep{cheng2024terahertz,wang2025selfinduced,zhang2023noiseless,chao2025tmoke}.
Every adaptation is pinned to an equation-provenance row (EP-$\ast$) with
a \emph{binding} forbidden-transfer clause; the reciprocity posture is
explicit: an unbiased passive quartz path is reciprocal, and the
pre-registered expectation for every directional observable is the null
(D6-003). The Hydrogenuine memory record \eqid{RSCS-C.15} and its
store/replay/update operators \eqid{RSCS-O.14--16} are \ENG{} software
abstractions; the NHT space-to-phase encoding \eqid{RSCS-O.3} and HAL
lattice remain \HYP{} and quarantined
\citep{arisaka2022nht,arisaka2022vision}.

\section{The Conservative Extension Property}\label{sec:cep}
The embedding $\iotamap$ maps every v2 state into its typed RSCS
counterpart. For each generalizing operator the battery checks
$O_{\mathrm{RSCS}}(\iotamap(x)) = \iotamap(O_{\mathrm{RGCS}}(x))$ with
rtol $10^{-9}$ / atol $10^{-12}$ (D3-011) across the frozen results
RGCS-M.23/24/28/46/55/56 and 10--11:
\input{tables/cep}
The battery runs in the test suite on every commit; a CEP failure is a
release blocker, not a warning.

\section{Identifiability, gauge, and uncertainty boundary}
Phase coordinates carry an $S^1$ gauge (a global phase is unobservable);
parity/supermode bases \eqid{RSCS-O.5} are unitary and self-inverse;
frame transforms \eqid{RSCS-O.1} declare their singularity handling.
Uncertainty \eqid{RSCS-C.12} is a coordinate, propagated by
\eqid{RSCS-O.11} \DER{} in agreement with the v2 propagation rules; no
RSCS operator may output a value stripped of its uncertainty when the
input carried one.

\section{Status and scope}
RSCS~1.0 is software and mathematics with machine evidence; it confirms
no new physics. Its hypotheses (H-20\,--\,H-30 in the claim register) are
pre-registered with observables, controls, and failure conditions --- and
several are pre-registered \emph{nulls}. The framework's value claim is
reproducibility: every number in this document was generated from the
tested libraries at build time.
